summary: The investigation confirms a case of industrial espionage involving the exfiltration of confidential PDF documents related to the Palomino product. Evidence indicates malware was installed on the victim's (Yob Taog) phone to facilitate systematic downloads and exfiltration to an external server (50.56.29.109). The suspect, Donald Norby, engaged in negotiations for the sale of these documents, including a failed renegotiation attempt to increase the price, which may be linked to the criminal group 'KRYPTIX'. Norby's subsequent death under violent circumstances suggests a potential organized crime nexus.

artifacts.name: Exfiltration Server
artifacts.description: Hardcoded external server IP found in malware code used for systematic PDF exfiltration.

artifacts.name: Malware Activity
artifacts.description: SMS messages confirm malware installation and periodic exfiltration of confidential documents.

artifacts.name: Confidential Documents
artifacts.description: Confidential SwiftLogic PDF files identified on the device SD card.

artifacts.name: Reconnaissance Activity
artifacts.description: Browser history indicates prior reconnaissance on target company employees.

artifacts.name: Device Identification
artifacts.description: EXIF metadata from photos on the device confirms the hardware as a Motorola Droid, linking the device to the exfiltrated files.

artifacts.name: Criminal Nexus
artifacts.description: Evidence of negotiation dynamics and suspect's violent death suggests involvement of the criminal group KRYPTIX.
